trades.sentiment_summary: Credo Technology Group (CRDO) released preliminary Q3 FY2026 revenue guidance of $404-408 million, far exceeding prior guidance of $335-345 million and consensus estimates of around $341-346 million, driving significant stock gains of 9-17% in recent sessions amid strong AI infrastructure demand[2][3][4][5]. The results highlight robust adoption of the company's high-speed connectivity solutions, best-in-class 67% gross margins, and a raised full-year growth target over 200% YoY, while quelling concerns over competitive threats like co-packaged optics[3][5]. Analyst support remains positive, with recent reaffirmations of Buy ratings and price targets up to $220[1][6].

BOSS Zhipin (BZ) announced on February 11, 2026, that it repurchased 650,180 ordinary shares for over RMB40 million on February 9-10, with nearly RMB260 million deployed on buybacks in 2026 so far. The stock gained 3.03% following the announcement, outperforming mixed performance from peer companies.

Brazilian fintech AGI (AGBK) priced its IPO at the low end of $12 per share on Feb. 10, 2026, after slashing deal size by 65% from original terms of 43.64 million shares at $15-$18 to raise $720 million. The company raised $240 million and began trading on NYSE on Feb. 11, 2026, amid a weak market following a 20% slide in peer PicPay (PICS). AGI reported profitability with $193 million net income on $1.86 billion revenue for the latest 12 months.

Match Group reported Q4 2025 earnings that beat analyst expectations on both revenue ($878 million, +0.74%) and EPS ($1.06, +5.37%), with improved margins and early signs of user engagement from Tinder features like DoubleDate and FaceCheck[1][2][4]. The company raised its quarterly dividend by 5% to $0.20 per share, continued aggressive share buybacks, and guided for modest Q1 2026 revenue growth of 2-3%[1][4]. After-hours trading on February 11 showed the stock up 1.60% to $31.12, amid flat 2026 revenue outlook[3].

The S&P/ASX 200 index rose 1.66% to a 3-month high, driven by strong gains in financials, gold, and utilities sectors, with top performers like AGL Energy up 11.64% on solid half-year results and tightened guidance.[2][3] CSL shares plunged 11.80% to 5-year lows due to a shock CEO exit announcement ahead of half-year results.[1][2] Positive notes include expectations of further ASX 200 gains and a buy rating on WiseTech shares as undervalued.[1]

State of Michigan Retirement System increased its APG position by 52.8%, while Oppenheimer Asset Management also bought additional shares, signaling institutional confidence. APG stock rose 1.07% to $45.29 on February 10, with elevated trading volume and positive technical signals like rising MACD. Analyst consensus remains 'Moderate Buy' with a $43.24 target, though one article highlights risks from weak operating margins.

Astera Labs reported strong Q4 2025 results, beating revenue estimates at $270.6M (up 92% YoY) and EPS at $0.58 vs. $0.51 expected, with above-consensus Q1 FY2026 guidance of $286M–$297M revenue. However, shares dropped 5-12% after hours due to margin concerns, operating leverage disappointment, and high valuation expectations. Positive developments include a new Israel R&D center and a warrant agreement with Amazon for up to $6.5B in products.

Century Aluminum (CENX) signed a power agreement extension with Santee Cooper through 2031 and plans a $50 million investment to restart idle capacity at its Mt. Holly smelter, adding 50,000 metric tons of annual production by early summer 2026 and creating over 100 jobs[2]. Analyst price targets for CENX were raised by 27.97% to an average of $51.89, with strong sentiment signals across all time horizons supporting an overweight bias[1][3]. The company will report Q4 2025 earnings on February 19, 2026[2][5].

Amkor Technology (AMKR) stock hit a new 52-week high above $55 following a strong Q4 earnings beat, with $1.89B revenue surpassing estimates and positive Q1 guidance above consensus, driven by AI demand and advanced packaging growth.[1][3][6] Analyst upgrades from Needham and JPMorgan raised price targets to $65 with Buy/Overweight ratings, fueling a 10%+ surge and bullish momentum.[1][2][6] Management highlighted robust 2026 computing growth over 20% and capacity expansions, though Q1 EPS guidance brackets consensus.[1][3]

Albertsons Companies (ACI) is scheduled to report earnings today, February 11, 2026, amid recent stock weakness with a 7.79% decline over the past five sessions and a 4-6 down weekly pattern. A technical analysis suggests a 62% historical probability of upside next week following such patterns, potentially targeting $21.36 from $20.53. Institutional buying by Alps Advisors (150k shares) and neutral insider dividend equivalent grants occurred, while analysts maintain a Hold/Buy consensus with a $21.58 target.

BILL Holdings reported strong Q2 fiscal 2026 results on February 5, beating revenue expectations with $414.7 million (up 14% YoY) and EPS of $0.64 versus $0.56 expected, while raising full-year guidance.[3][4][8] The stock surged 37% post-earnings to around $49, with analysts upgrading targets up to $77 and maintaining a Moderate Buy consensus around $56-$61.[1][2][3] Recent coverage highlights profitability improvements, growth momentum, and significant upside potential despite some mixed after-hours volatility.[1][2][5]

Axalta reported record full-year 2025 earnings with an Adjusted EBITDA margin of 22%, demonstrating strong operational performance despite a challenging macro environment.[1] However, fourth quarter net sales declined 4% year-over-year due to lower volumes, and 2026 guidance projects only low-to-mid single-digit revenue growth alongside a high debt load that constrains future earnings expansion.[1][3]

Bombardier Inc. Class B (TSE:BBD.B) stock crossed above its 200-day moving average of C$205.13, reaching a high of C$254.79 and closing at C$250.53 on February 10, 2026, indicating positive price momentum[1]. A technical analysis video for BCM Resources Corporation (B stock) highlighted a bearish candle on February 10 with support at 19 cents and resistance at 22 cents[2]. No other significant news directly impacting a unified 'B stock' appeared in the last 24 hours, with mentions of unrelated stocks like Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.B) and Mattel (MAT)[4][3].

Best Buy (BBY) recently reported quarterly EPS of $1.40, beating estimates of $1.31, with revenue up 2.4% to $9.67B, and raised FY2026 EPS guidance to $6.25-$6.35. Analyst consensus remains a Buy rating with a mean price target of $80.58, implying 20% upside, though the stock has declined 21% over 52 weeks amid retail sector underperformance. Recent trading shows volatility, with a 4.6% drop noted alongside institutional buying like ING Groep NV adding shares.
